title: sanguisorba officinalis subsp. glandulosa
intro: Sanguisorba officinalis subsp. glandulosa, also known as Burnet, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the rose family native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its reddish-purple flowers and edible leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Sanguisorba officinalis subsp. glandulosa include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions.
